papers.title: The Iconic-Symbolic Spectrum
papers.abstract: A theory of iconic and symbolic representation.

papers.title: Varieties of Iconicity
papers.abstract: An overview of the varieties of iconic representational systems and their functional properties.

papers.title: The Structure of Visual Content
papers.abstract: A structured theory of visual content, in which clusters of objects and properties populate direction-filled visual arrays.

papers.title: Visual Objects
papers.abstract: Visual singular contents are intentional objects, not concrete referents or perceptual modes of presentation.

papers.title: Neural Images: On Retinotopy and Representation
papers.abstract: An argument for the iconic status of retinotopic regions, based on analogies between convolutional neural networks and visual processing in the brain.

papers.title: Map Semantics and the Geography of Meaning
papers.citation: In Ernest Lepore & Luvell Anderson (eds.), The Oxford Handbook of Applied Philosophy of Language. Oxford University Press, pp. 489-522 (2024)
papers.abstract: Situates maps within the broader landscape of semiotic meaning, then develops a projection-based semantics for maps, focusing on space, lines, and labels.

papers.title: Review of Modeling the Meanings of Pictures
papers.abstract: Review of John Kulvicki's 2021 book on pictorial meaning.

papers.title: Semantics of Pictorial Space
papers.abstract: Foundations of projection-based semantics for pictorial representation.

papers.title: Tagging: semantics at the iconic/symbolic interface
papers.citation: In Julian J. Schlöder, Dean McHugh & Floris Roelofsen (eds.), Proceedings of the 22nd Amsterdam Colloquium, pp. 11-20 (2019)
papers.abstract: A compositional semantic analysis of tagging, in which regions of a picture are annotated with words or other symbols.

papers.title: Content and Target in Pictorial Representation
papers.abstract: Introduces a view of pictorial representation which distinguishes a picture's target, its singular content, and argues against descriptivist theories of singular content.

papers.title: Beyond Resemblance
papers.abstract: An extended argument against resemblance-based theories of depiction, and in favor of a projection-based approach.

papers.title: Spatial Coherence in Narrative Film
papers.abstract: A theory of film space: its structure, what makes it coherent, and how spatial coherence interacts with viewpoint constraints.

papers.title: Showing Seeing in Film
papers.coauthors: with Samuel Cumming, Elsi Kaiser, and Rory Kelly
papers.abstract: An investigation of two film conventions for representing what a character sees: point-of-view (POV) and sight link; an extension of our project on viewpoint constraints in film.

papers.title: Conventions of Viewpoint Coherence in Film
papers.coauthors: with Samuel Cumming and Rory Kelly
papers.abstract: A defense of a semantic view of film, as against a purely pragmatic view, and a close analysis of two semantic conventions: the X-Constraint (a.k.a. the 180° rule) and the T-Constraint.
papers.extraHtml: A video essay by Koby Leff based on this paper.

papers.title: A semantics of face emoji in discourse
papers.coauthors: with Patrick Georg Grosz, Christian De Leon, and Elsi Kaiser
papers.abstract: A semantic account of the contribution of face emoji to linguistic discourse.

papers.title: Counterfactuals and modality
papers.abstract: An argument for the strict analysis of counterfactual conditionals, and against the variable analysis, based on interactions between subjunctive modals and counterfactual conditionals.

papers.title: Seven Puzzles of Pictorial Representation
papers.abstract: A structured theory of visual content, in which clusters of objects and properties populate direction-filled visual arrays.

papers.title: Special issue, "Pictorial and Diagrammatic Representation"
papers.contentsHtml:
1. Introduction: Varieties of Iconicity
    Valeria Giardino and Gabriel Greenberg
  2. Wayfinding: Notes on the 'Public' as Interactive
    Patrick Maynard
  3. The Mystery of Deduction and Diagrammatic Aspects of Representation
    Sun-Joo Shin
  4. Meaning and Demonstration
    Matthew Stone and Una Stojnic
  5. The Cognitive Design of Tools of Thought
    Barbara Tversky
  6. Diagrams as Tools for Scientific Reasoning
    Adele Abrahamsen and William Bechtel
  7. Street Signs and Ikea Instruction Sheets: Pragmatics and Pictorial Communication
    Marcello Frixione and Antonio Lombardi
  8. Pictures Have Propositional Content
    Alex Grzankowski
  9. Analog Representation and the Parts Principle
    John Kulvicki
  10. Trompe l'oeil and the Dorsal/Ventral Account of Pictorial Perception
    Bence Nanay
